18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}
45	
46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}
104	
105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}

276	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
277	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
278	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
279	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
280		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
281		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
282		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → NoChangesContract default
283		// VerificationRunner runs a verification_command parsed out of a
284		// no_changes_rationale.txt under NoChangesContract (TD-031 §8.1). When
285		// nil, DefaultVerificationCommandRunner is used.
286		VerificationRunner VerificationCommandRunner
287		Now                func() time.Time
288		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
289		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
290		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
291		Reviewer BeadReviewer
292	
293		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
294		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
295		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
296		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
297		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
298		// (escalating to land_conflict_needs_human); false means failed-but-retriable.
299		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
300	
301		// ComplexityGate, when non-nil, is invoked between RoutePreflight and Claim.
302		// It evaluates whether the candidate bead is atomic (proceed to Claim),
303		// decomposable (file children, block parent, re-pick), or ambiguous
304		// (surface to human triage). When nil, the gate is bypassed and a one-time
305		// warning is emitted per Run() invocation. See triage.go and
306		// NewComplexityGate for the standard implementation.
307		ComplexityGate TriageGate
308	
309		// conflictAutoRecoverFn replaces the default landConflictAutoRecover. Set
310		// in tests to inject controlled recovery results without a real git repo.
311		conflictAutoRecoverFn func(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error)
312	}

349	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
350		if w.Store == nil {
351			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
352		}
353		if w.Executor == nil {
354			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
355		}
356	
357		now := w.Now
358		if now == nil {
359			now = time.Now
360		}
361		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
362		if assignee == "" {
363			assignee = "ddx"
364		}
365		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
366		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
367			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
368		}
369		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
370		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
371			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
372		}
373		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
374		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
375			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
376		}
377		harness := rcfg.Harness()
378		model := rcfg.Model()
379		profile := rcfg.Profile()
380	
381		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
382		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
383		// triagedWarned suppresses the one-time boot warning when ComplexityGate is
384		// nil. One warning per Run() invocation is enough; the bool resets on the
385		// next worker boot.
386		triagedWarned := false
387		// hookFailed tracks beads whose pre-claim hook failed on first presentation
388		// in this run. A bead in hookFailed but not attempted gets one retry: on the
389		// second hook failure it moves to attempted so nextCandidate will skip it and
390		// the loop can exit gracefully. This prevents infinite spinning when the hook
391		// always fails while still allowing transient hook failures to be retried.
392		hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{})
393	
394		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
395			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
396		}
397	
398		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
399			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
400			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
401			"harness":      harness,
402			"model":        model,
403			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
404			"assignee":     assignee,
405			"once":         runtime.Once,
406		})
407		defer func() {
408			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
409				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
410				"successes":           result.Successes,
411				"failures":            result.Failures,
412				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
413			})
414		}()
415	
416		for {
417			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
418			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
419			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
420			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
421			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
422				return result, err
423			}
424	
425			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
426			if err != nil {
427				return result, err
428			}
429			if !ok {
430				if result.Attempts == 0 {
431					result.NoReadyWork = true
432					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
433						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
434							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
435								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
436								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
437								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
438								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
439								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
440							}
441						}
442					}
443				}
444				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
445					return result, nil
446				}
447				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
448					return result, err
449				}
450				continue
451			}
452	
453			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
454			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
455			// continues (ctx is not cancelled). The bead is NOT added to
456			// attempted on the FIRST failure so a transient hook failure (e.g.
457			// diverged branch) allows a retry on the next iteration. On a
458			// SECOND failure for the same bead in the same run it is moved into
459			// attempted so nextCandidate will skip it, preventing an infinite
460			// loop when the hook always fails.
461			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
462				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
463					if runtime.Log != nil {
464						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
465					}
466					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
467						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
468						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
469					})
470					if _, alreadyFailed := hookFailed[candidate.ID]; alreadyFailed {
471						// Second failure in this run: stop retrying this bead.
472						attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
473					} else {
474						hookFailed[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
475					}
476					continue
477				}
478			}
479	
480			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
481	
482			// Routing preflight gate (FEAT-006 D3, ddx-98e6e9ef): consult the
483			// upstream typed-incompatibility surface BEFORE claiming. If the
484			// configured (harness, model) cannot serve the bead, exit the loop
485			// with a worker-level failure record — no claim, no executor
486			// invocation, no tier-attempt event burn.
487			if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
488				if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
489					detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
490						harness, model, rerr.Error())
491					if runtime.Log != nil {
492						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (skipping %s, exiting loop)\n",
493							detail, candidate.ID)
494					}
495					emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
496						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
497						"harness": harness,
498						"model":   model,
499						"reason":  rerr.Error(),
500					})
501					report := ExecuteBeadReport{
502						BeadID:  candidate.ID,
503						Status:  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
504						Detail:  detail,
505						Harness: harness,
506						Model:   model,
507					}
508					result.Attempts++
509					result.Failures++
510					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
511					result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
512					return result, nil
513				}
514			}
515	
516			// Complexity triage gate (ddx-5bf4ee7e): pre-Claim decomposition check.
517			// When the gate is nil, decomposition responsibility shifts to the
518			// LLM-driven path inside execute-bead (ddx-b790449b AC5): the agent
519			// is prompted to recognize coarse beads, file children, and exit
520			// early. The pre-dispatch warning is intentionally suppressed —
521			// it is no longer accurate now that decomposition lives downstream.
522			if w.ComplexityGate == nil {
523				_ = triagedWarned // retained for future re-introduction of a typed gate
524			} else {
525				shouldClaim, triageErr := w.ComplexityGate(ctx, candidate)
526				if triageErr != nil {
527					if runtime.Log != nil {
528						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log,
529							"triage gate error for %s: %v (skipping)\n", candidate.ID, triageErr)
530					}
531					emit("triage.error", map[string]any{
532						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
533						"reason":  triageErr.Error(),
534					})
535					// A gate error does not count as an attempt; re-pick next candidate.
536					continue
537				}
538				if !shouldClaim {
539					if runtime.Log != nil {
540						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log,
541							"triage: skipped dispatch of %s (classified as non-atomic)\n", candidate.ID)
542					}
543					emit("triage.skipped", map[string]any{"bead_id": candidate.ID})
544					continue
545				}
546			}
547	
548			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
549				continue
550			}
551	
552			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
553				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
554				"title":    candidate.Title,
555				"assignee": assignee,
556			})
557	
558			if runtime.Log != nil {
559				if candidate.Title != "" {
560					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
561				} else {
562					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
563				}
564			}
565	
566			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
567			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
568			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
569			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
570			runStart := now()
571			phaseSeq := 0
572			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
573				phaseSeq++
574				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
575					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
576					harness, model, profile,
577					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
578				))
579			}
580	
581			nextPhase("queueing", false)
582	
583			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
584			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
585			hbWG.Add(1)
586			go func(beadID string) {
587				defer hbWG.Done()
588				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
589				defer ticker.Stop()
590				for {
591					select {
592					case <-hbCtx.Done():
593						return
594					case <-ticker.C:
595						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
596					}
597				}
598			}(candidate.ID)
599	
600			nextPhase("running", false)

9	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
10	// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
11	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
12	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
13	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
14	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
15		cmd := &cobra.Command{
16			Use:   "work",
17			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
18			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
19	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
20	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
21	
22	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
23	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
24	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
25	within the requested power bounds.
26	
27	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
28	  drained     — no ready beads remain
29	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
30	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
31	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
32	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
33	
34	By default work submits to the running ddx server as a background worker and
35	returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current process.
36	`,
37			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
38	  ddx work
39

53		}

1581	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoop(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {

1585	// runAgentExecuteLoopImpl is the shared implementation for runAgentExecuteLoop
1586	// and runWork. When treatPassthroughAsOpaque is true (ddx work path),
1587	// harness/provider/model are passed to Execute unchanged and
1588	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
1589	// branch on their values. When false (ddx agent execute-loop path), the
1590	// pre-flight validation gate runs first.
1591	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd *cobra.Command, treatPassthroughAsOpaque bool) error {
1592		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
1593		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
1594		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
1595		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
1596		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
1597		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
1598		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
1599		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
1600		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
1601		once, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("once")
1602		pollInterval, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("poll-interval")
1603		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
1604		local, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local")
1605		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
1606		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
1607		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
1608		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
1609		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
1610		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
1611		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
1612	
1613		// Zero-config auto-route: when the operator passes no routing flags and
1614		// the project has no .ddx/config.yaml routing block, dispatch flows
1615		// through fizeau's provider resolution. Provider configuration and any
1616		// "no providers configured" error reporting is fizeau's concern; ddx
1617		// does not read fizeau's config files.
1618		//
1619		// ddx-b2c9a245: tier is inferred per bead via escalation.InferTier in
1620		// the executor closure below when no routing flags are present.
1621		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" && modelRef == "" &&
1622			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile")
1623		autoInferTier := noRoutingFlags && !projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
1624	
1625		// If --local, run inline; otherwise submit to running ddx server
1626		if !local {
1627			return f.executeLoopWithServer(cmd, projectRoot, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once, pollInterval, asJSON, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, treatPassthroughAsOpaque)
1628		}
1629	
1630		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1631		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1632		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1633		// any bead status changes hands.
1634		// Skipped for ddx work (treatPassthroughAsOpaque=true): harness/provider/model
1635		// are opaque passthrough constraints there; DDx does not validate them.
1636		if !treatPassthroughAsOpaque {
1637			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
1638				return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
1639			}

17	// claimCountingStore wraps bead.Store and counts Claim invocations. The rest
18	// of ExecuteBeadLoopStore is delegated to the embedded *bead.Store.
19	type claimCountingStore struct {
20		*bead.Store
21		claimCalls int32
22	}
23	
24	func (s *claimCountingStore) Claim(id, assignee string) error {
25		atomic.AddInt32(&s.claimCalls, 1)
26		return s.Store.Claim(id, assignee)
27	}
28	
29	func (s *claimCountingStore) Heartbeat(id string) error            { return s.Store.Heartbeat(id) }
30	func (s *claimCountingStore) Unclaim(id string) error              { return s.Store.Unclaim(id) }

51	// TestRoutingPreflightRejectionExitsLoopWithoutClaim covers ddx-98e6e9ef:
52	// when RoutePreflight returns the upstream typed-incompatibility error
53	// (agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible), the loop must NOT claim the bead,
54	// must NOT invoke the executor, must record a worker-level execution_failed
55	// result naming the rejected (harness, model) pair, and must exit.
56	func TestRoutingPreflightRejectionExitsLoopWithoutClaim(t *testing.T) {
57		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
58		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
59	
60		var execCount int32
61		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
62			Store: store,
63			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
64				atomic.AddInt32(&execCount, 1)
65				return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess}, nil
66			}),
67		}
68	
69		// Fabricate a typed-incompatibility error via the upstream test seam:
70		// the preflight callback returns the same error type that upstream
71		// ResolveRoute returns when a harness allow-list rejects the model.
72		rejected := agentlib.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible{
73			Harness:         "claude",
74			Model:           "gpt-5",
75			SupportedModels: []string{"claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
76		}
77		var preflightCalls int32
78		preflight := func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
79			atomic.AddInt32(&preflightCalls, 1)
80			return rejected
81		}
82	
83		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
84			Assignee: "worker",
85			Harness:  "claude",
86			Model:    "gpt-5",
87		}
88		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
89	
90		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
91			Once:           true,
92			RoutePreflight: preflight,
93		})
94		require.NoError(t, err)
95		require.NotNil(t, result)
96	
97		// AC#1: preflight ran before Claim.
98		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightCalls), "preflight must be invoked")
99	
100		// AC#2: Claim was NEVER called on the store.
101		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls),
102			"beadStore.Claim must not be called when preflight rejects")
103	
104		// Executor must not have been invoked either.
105		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCount),
106			"executor must not run when preflight rejects")
107	
108		// AC#3: worker-level execution_failed record names the rejected pair.
109		require.Len(t, result.Results, 1, "exactly one worker-level failure record")
110		report := result.Results[0]
111		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, report.Status)
112		assert.Equal(t, candidate.ID, report.BeadID)
113		assert.Equal(t, "claude", report.Harness)
114		assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5", report.Model)
115		assert.Contains(t, report.Detail, "claude", "detail must name rejected harness")
116		assert.Contains(t, report.Detail, "gpt-5", "detail must name rejected model")
117		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, result.LastFailureStatus)
118		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures)
119	
120		// AC#4: bead has no kind:tier-attempt event from this attempt.
121		events, err := store.Events(candidate.ID)
122		require.NoError(t, err)
123		for _, ev := range events {
124			assert.NotEqual(t, "tier-attempt", ev.Kind,
125				"no tier-attempt event must be recorded when preflight rejects pre-claim")
126		}
127	
128		// Bead must remain open with no owner — confirms no Claim side-effects.
129		got, err := store.Get(candidate.ID)
130		require.NoError(t, err)
131		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
132		assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
133	}
